creator: m.viey@wanadoo.fr
text:
the 3 libs are installed ffmpeg as a FF dependency, and the others as dependencies of the old cmus from port.

mik@labo $ pkg info -fdr audio/libmodplug 
libmodplug-0.8.9.1.r461
Name           : libmodplug
Version        : 0.8.9.1.r461
Installed on   : Wed Mar  8 09:39:53 2023 CET
Origin         : audio/libmodplug
Architecture   : FreeBSD:14:amd64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : audio
Licenses       : PD
Maintainer     : ehaupt@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : http://modplug-xmms.sourceforge.net/
Comment        : ModPlug mod-like music shared libraries
Shared Libs provided:
	libmodplug.so.1
Annotations    :
	FreeBSD_version: 1400081
	cpe            : cpe:2.3:a:konstanty_bialkowski:libmodplug:0.8.9.1.r461:::::freebsd14:x64
Flat size      : 379KiB
Description    :
This is a library based on the mod rendering code from ModPlug, a
popular Windows mod player written by Olivier Lapicque, found at
http://www.modplug.com.  It is required for ModPlug-XMMS (in the
xmms-modplug package).

WWW: http://modplug-xmms.sourceforge.net/

09-04-23 13:30:41 /home/mik
mik@labo $ pkg info -fdr audio/sndio     
sndio-1.9.0
Name           : sndio
Version        : 1.9.0
Installed on   : Sat Oct 22 14:10:20 2022 CEST
Origin         : audio/sndio
Architecture   : FreeBSD:14:amd64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : audio
Licenses       : ISCL
Maintainer     : multimedia@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : https://www.sndio.org/
Comment        : Small audio and MIDI framework from the OpenBSD project
Shared Libs provided:
	libsndio.so.7.2
Annotations    :
	FreeBSD_version: 1400073
Flat size      : 258KiB
Description    :
Sndio is a small audio and MIDI framework part of the OpenBSD project.

It provides an lightweight audio & MIDI server and a fully documented
user-space API to access either the server or directly the hardware in
a uniform way.  Sndio is designed to work for desktop applications,
but pays special attention to synchronization mechanisms and
reliability required by music applications.  Reliability through
simplicity are part of the project goals.

WWW: https://www.sndio.org/



what I don't get is that none of them is in charge of displaying the UI, it's rather the job of ncurses.

Something else, I have warning in cmus about pulseaudio which is built without capabilities, I think I had the issue before when I tried to build from port. As far I remember I tried to reconfigure pulseaudio port with the capability option, but I didn't find it.
